spec_match.rationale: Names and exercises `NavigatorDeviceMemory`, `navigator.deviceMemory`, WorkerNavigator exposure, `Sec-CH-Device-Memory`, and Chrome 147's implementation-defined Android/desktop bucket limits.

interactive.rationale: Live-read and worker-probe buttons invoke the API, while platform radios, RAM controls, presets, and fallback mode update the bucket chart and app decision in-page.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page now reads the live value and visualizes the old versus Chrome 147 bucket systems promised by the feature index blurb.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers unsupported API fallback, secure-context status, worker exposure absence, low/high implementation clamps, and power-of-two tie rounding.

references.rationale: The see-also section links directly to https://chromestatus.com/feature/6330376953921536 and the Device Memory API spec.

design_system.rationale: All local colors use shared CSS variables, button background overrides also set color, and the page is no longer a static code-card.

summary: The memory inspector now clears the major critique by providing real controls, live API probes, spec-named readouts, bucket-limit simulation, and honest fallback behavior.
